childhood.birthPlace: Jackson Heights, Queens, New York City
childhood.familyBackground: Italian-American family; father Marino Dallolio was a jazz musician; mother Ann was a homemaker; working-class roots in immigrant community; moved to Manhasset, Long Island at age 8
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 이탈리아계 미국인 가정으로, 아버지 마리노는 재즈 뮤지션, 어머니 앤은 전업주부였으며, 이민자 공동체에서 노동자 계급의 뿌리를 가짐. 8세에 롱아일랜드 맨하셋으로 이주
childhood.education: C.W. Post College, B.S. in Finance (1971); Harvard Business School, MBA (1973); early investment education from working as golf caddy and being introduced to Wall Street professionals as a teenager
childhood.educationKo: C.W. 포스트 대학 금융학 학사(1971년), 하버드 경영대학원 MBA(1973년). 청소년 시절 골프 캐디로 일하며 월스트리트 전문가들을 만나 실무적 투자 교육을 받음
childhood.earlyLife: Born 1949; began investing at age 12, buying shares of Northeast Airlines for $300 (tripled investment when airline merged); worked as golf caddy on Long Island where he met Wall Street professionals who inspired his interest in markets; showed early entrepreneurial drive and curiosity about financial markets; grew up in post-WWII America during economic expansion and relative prosperity
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 1949년 태어났으며, 12세에 투자를 시작하여 노스이스트항공 주식을 300달러에 매수(항공사 합병 후 3배 상승). 롱아일랜드에서 골프 캐디로 일하며 월스트리트 전문가들을 만나 시장에 대한 관심을 키워짐. 초기부터 기업가 정신과 금융시장에 대한 호기심 발휘. 전후 미국의 경제 확장과 상대적 풍요로움 속에서 성장
childhood.source: Wikipedia, Bridgewater Associates official biography, Academy of Achievement, CNBC

careerTimeline.event: Graduated from C.W. Post College with B.S. in Finance at age 22
careerTimeline.eventKo: 22세 나이에 C.W. 포스트 대학 금융학 학사 졸업

careerTimeline.event: Received MBA from Harvard Business School; began trading commodities and working on New York Stock Exchange floor
careerTimeline.eventKo: 하버드 경영대학원 MBA 취득, 상품 선물거래 시작 및 뉴욕 증권거래소 펀드에서 일하기 시작

careerTimeline.event: Worked as futures trader and broker at Shearson Hayden Stone, advising farmers and cattle ranchers on hedging; fired after punching boss at New Year's Eve party while drunk
careerTimeline.eventKo: 셔슨 헤이든 스톤에서 선물거래자 및 중개인으로 일하며 농민과 축산업자에게 헤징 자문 제공. 신년전야 파티에서 음주 상태로 상사를 폭행하여 해고됨

careerTimeline.event: Founded Bridgewater Associates from two-bedroom apartment in Westport, Connecticut with initial focus on wealth advisory services
careerTimeline.eventKo: 코네티컷 웨스트포트 원룸 아파트에서 브릿지워터 어소시에이츠를 설립하여 자산관리 자문 사업을 시작

careerTimeline.event: Experienced catastrophic losses betting on depression that never occurred; went broke and borrowed $4,000 from father to pay family bills; forced to lay off all employees; learned humility and developed systematic approach to investing
careerTimeline.eventKo: 경기 침체 예측에 베팅했으나 실제로는 장기 강세장이 시작되어 대규모 손실 발생. 파산하여 아버지로부터 4천 달러를 빌려 가족생계 유지. 모든 직원을 해고해야 함. 이를 통해 겸손함을 배우고 체계적 투자 접근법 개발

careerTimeline.event: Began coding investment principles into computer algorithms and backtesting strategies; transformed Bridgewater from discretionary to systematic trading
careerTimeline.eventKo: 투자 원칙을 컴퓨터 알고리즘으로 코딩하고 과거 데이터를 바탕으로 검증 시작. 브릿지워터를 재량거래에서 체계적 거래로 전환

careerTimeline.event: Bridgewater's Pure Alpha strategy begins generating consistent returns through 1992; fund starts gaining institutional investor interest
careerTimeline.eventKo: 브릿지워터의 순수알파 전략이 1990년부터 1992년까지 지속적인 수익 창출 시작. 기관투자자들의 관심이 증가하기 시작

careerTimeline.event: Introduced 'radical transparency' culture at Bridgewater after being told by three top managers he was hurting firm through excessive honesty; created environment for 'thoughtful disagreement'
careerTimeline.eventKo: 3명의 고위 임원으로부터 과도한 솔직함이 회사에 해를 끼친다는 지적을 받은 후 '급진적 투명성' 문화 도입. '신중한 의견 대립' 환경 조성

careerTimeline.event: Bridgewater Associates becomes world's largest hedge fund by assets under management; Dalio's personal wealth reaches $15+ billion
careerTimeline.eventKo: 브릿지워터 어소시에이츠가 운용자산 기준으로 세계 최대 헤지펀드가 됨. 달리오 개인 자산이 150억 달러 이상에 도달

careerTimeline.event: Self-published Principles (123 pages) outlining philosophy of investment and corporate management
careerTimeline.eventKo: 투자 철학과 기업경영 원칙을 담은 'Principles' 자비출판(123페이지)

careerTimeline.event: Published bestseller 'Principles: Life and Work'; book becomes influential text on decision-making and organizational management
careerTimeline.eventKo: '원칙: 삶과 일(Principles: Life and Work)' 베스트셀러 출간. 의사결정과 조직관리에 영향력 있는 저서가 됨

careerTimeline.event: Published 'Principles for Navigating Big Debt Crises' with detailed case studies of 48 debt crises over past century
careerTimeline.eventKo: '대규모 채무위기 극복 원칙' 출간. 지난 100년간 발생한 48개 채무위기 사례연구 포함

careerTimeline.event: Bridgewater's Pure Alpha II fund drops 18.6%; worst performance in a decade; $3.5 billion investor outflows due to two consecutive years of computer model failures
careerTimeline.eventKo: 브릿지워터 순수알파 II 펀드가 18.6% 하락. 10년 만에 최악의 성과. 컴퓨터 모델 오류로 인한 2년 연속 손실로 35억 달러 규모 투자자 이탈

careerTimeline.event: Published 'Principles for Dealing with the Changing World Order' analyzing global power shifts and economic cycles
careerTimeline.eventKo: 글로벌 권력 이동과 경제사이클을 분석한 '변화하는 세계질서 극복 원칙' 출간

failures.description: Catastrophic loss from betting heavily that U.S. economy would enter depression; made public proclamations in newspapers, TV, and Congressional testimony; stock market instead began 18-year bull run; Dalio went broke and had to borrow $4,000 from father; forced to lay off all employees
failures.descriptionKo: 미국 경기 침체 예측에 무겁게 베팅했으나, 신문, TV, 의회 증언을 통해 공개적으로 예측을 발표한 후 주식시장은 18년간의 강세장이 시작됨. 달리오는 파산하여 아버지로부터 4천 달러를 빌려야 했고, 모든 직원을 해고해야 함
failures.lesson: Humility and diversification are essential; no investor can predict economic outcomes with certainty; acknowledged his audacity needed to be tempered with humility; led to development of systematic approach and coded decision criteria that could be backtested
failures.lessonKo: 겸손함과 분산투자는 필수. 어떤 투자자도 경제 결과를 확실히 예측할 수 없음. 자신의 대담함이 겸손함으로 조절되어야 함을 깨달음. 이는 체계적 접근법과 백테스트 가능한 코딩된 의사결정 기준 개발로 이어짐
failures.source: CNBC interview, Fortune article, multiple biographical sources

failures.description: Bridgewater's Pure Alpha II fund declined 18.6% in 2020 despite being one of world's largest and most respected hedge funds; computer models misread markets for second consecutive year; triggered $3.5 billion in investor withdrawals during first seven months of 2020
failures.descriptionKo: 세계 최대 규모의 존경받는 헤지펀드임에도 불구하고 브릿지워터 순수알파 II 펀드가 2020년 18.6% 하락. 컴퓨터 모델이 2년 연속으로 시장을 잘못 읽음. 2020년 처음 7개월 동안 35억 달러 규모의 투자자 이탈 초래
failures.lesson: Even systematic, algorithmic approaches fail when market regimes change unexpectedly; macro environment shifts can overwhelm even sophisticated models; organization must maintain adaptability; indicated need for recalibration of models and management approach
failures.lessonKo: 체계적이고 알고리즘 기반 접근법도 시장 환경이 예기치 않게 변할 때 실패할 수 있음. 거시경제 환경의 변화는 정교한 모델도 압도할 수 있음. 조직은 적응성을 유지해야 함. 모델과 경영 접근법의 재조정 필요성을 시사

failures.description: Lost job at Shearson Hayden Stone after punching boss in the face while drunk at New Year's Eve party; incident marked low point of early career
failures.descriptionKo: 신년전야 파티에서 음주 상태로 상사를 폭행하여 셔슨 헤이든 스톤에서 직업을 잃음. 초기 경력의 최저점을 기록
failures.lesson: Personal discipline and emotional regulation are critical to professional success; demonstrated need for better coping mechanisms, eventually turning to transcendental meditation as solution
failures.lessonKo: 개인의 규율과 감정 조절은 직업적 성공에 매우 중요. 더 나은 대처 방식의 필요성을 깨달으며, 결국 해결책으로 명상을 실천하기 시작

quotes.text: Principles are fundamental truths that serve as the foundations for behavior that gets you what you want out of life. They can be applied again and again in similar situations to help you achieve your goals.
quotes.textKo: 원칙은 당신이 인생에서 원하는 것을 얻는 행동의 기초가 되는 근본적 진리이다. 이들은 유사한 상황에서 반복적으로 적용되어 당신의 목표 달성을 도울 수 있다.
quotes.context: Core definition of his life philosophy and the central concept of his major works
quotes.contextKo: 그의 인생 철학의 핵심 정의이자 주요 저작의 중심 개념

quotes.text: Think for yourself to decide 1) what you want, 2) what is true, and 3) what you should do to achieve #1 in light of #2 with humility and open-mindedness so that you consider the best thinking available to you.
quotes.textKo: 스스로 생각하여 결정하라: 1) 당신이 원하는 것이 무엇인지, 2) 무엇이 진실인지, 3) 겸손함과 개방적 마음으로 당신이 이용할 수 있는 최고의 사고를 고려하여 1번을 달성하기 위해 무엇을 해야 하는지.
quotes.context: His first principle for decision-making and personal development
quotes.contextKo: 의사결정과 개인 발전을 위한 첫 번째 원칙

quotes.text: To be effective you must not let your need to be right be more important than your need to find out what's true.
quotes.textKo: 효과적이려면 옳다는 필요성이 진실을 찾으려는 필요성보다 더 중요하지 않아야 한다.
quotes.context: Management and decision-making principle emphasizing intellectual humility
quotes.contextKo: 지적 겸손함을 강조하는 경영과 의사결정 원칙

quotes.text: Make your passion and your work one and the same and do it with people you want to be with.
quotes.textKo: 당신의 열정과 일을 같은 것으로 만들고 함께하고 싶은 사람들과 그것을 하라.
quotes.context: Core work principle about alignment of personal passion with professional mission
quotes.contextKo: 개인의 열정과 직업적 사명의 일치에 관한 핵심 업무 원칙

quotes.text: If you're not failing, you're not pushing your limits, and if you're not pushing your limits, you're not maximizing your potential.
quotes.textKo: 실패하지 않으면 한계를 밀어붙이지 않는 것이고, 한계를 밀어붙이지 않으면 잠재력을 극대화하지 않는 것이다.
quotes.context: Philosophy on learning through failure and continuous improvement
quotes.contextKo: 실패를 통한 학습과 지속적 개선에 관한 철학

quotes.text: Smart people are the ones who ask the most thoughtful questions, as opposed to thinking they have all the answers. Great questions are a much better indicator of future success than great answers.
quotes.textKo: 똑똑한 사람은 모든 답을 알고 있다고 생각하는 사람이 아니라 가장 신중한 질문을 하는 사람이다. 훌륭한 질문은 훌륭한 답변보다 미래의 성공을 훨씬 더 잘 나타낸다.
quotes.context: Insight on talent assessment and learning orientation
quotes.contextKo: 인재 평가와 학습 지향성에 대한 통찰

quotes.text: Money is a byproduct of excellence, not a goal. Our overriding objective is excellence and constant improvement at Bridgewater.
quotes.textKo: 돈은 목표가 아니라 우수성의 부산물이다. 브릿지워터의 궁극적 목표는 우수성과 지속적인 개선이다.
quotes.context: Statement of organizational values prioritizing excellence over profit
quotes.contextKo: 이익보다 우수성을 우선시하는 조직 가치관 선언

quotes.text: Going broke in 1982 was the best thing that ever happened to me because it gave me the humility to go along with my audacity.
quotes.textKo: 1982년에 파산한 것은 내게 일어난 최고의 일이다. 왜냐하면 그것이 내 대담함을 함께할 겸손함을 주었기 때문이다.
quotes.context: Reflection on transformative power of failure and humility
quotes.contextKo: 실패와 겸손함의 변혁적 힘에 대한 성찰

authored.title: Principles (self-published)
authored.note: First version published 2011 (123 pages); outlined investment philosophy and corporate management principles that became foundation for later bestseller
authored.noteKo: 2011년 초판 발행(123페이지). 투자 철학과 기업경영 원칙을 체계화하여 이후 베스트셀러의 기초가 됨

authored.title: Principles: Life and Work
authored.note: 2017 New York Times bestseller; comprehensive exploration of decision-making principles for life and work; sold over 5 million copies worldwide; became influential management text
authored.noteKo: 2017년 뉴욕 타임스 베스트셀러. 삶과 일의 의사결정 원칙을 종합적으로 탐구. 전 세계 500만 부 이상 판매. 영향력 있는 경영 저서로 자리 잡음

authored.title: Principles for Navigating Big Debt Crises
authored.note: 2018 publication; provided template for understanding debt cycles through analysis of 48 historical cases including 2008 financial crisis, Great Depression, and Weimar hyperinflation
authored.noteKo: 2018년 출간. 2008 금융위기, 대공황, 바이마르 하이퍼인플레이션을 포함한 48개 역사적 채무위기 사례분석을 통해 채무 순환을 이해하는 틀 제시

authored.title: Principles for Dealing with the Changing World Order
authored.note: 2021 publication analyzing major shifts in global power dynamics, economic systems, and geopolitical trends over centuries
authored.noteKo: 2021년 출간. 수 세기에 걸친 글로벌 권력 역학, 경제 체제, 지정학적 트렌드의 주요 변화를 분석

authored.title: Principles for Success
authored.note: 2019 illustrated version of core principles; made the concepts more accessible to broader audience
authored.noteKo: 2019년 출간한 핵심 원칙의 삽화본. 개념을 더 많은 대중이 접근 가능하게 만듦

recommended.title: The Economic Machine Works (animated video course)
recommended.author: Ray Dalio
recommended.why: Simplified explanation of macroeconomic principles that underlie his investment philosophy and systematic thinking about economies
recommended.whyKo: 그의 투자 철학과 경제에 대한 체계적 사고의 근간이 되는 거시경제 원칙을 단순화하여 설명

recommended.title: A Random Walk Down Wall Street
recommended.author: Burton Malkiel
recommended.why: Classic text on efficient market hypothesis and portfolio theory relevant to Dalio's development of systematic, diversified investment approaches
recommended.whyKo: 효율적 시장 가설과 포트폴리오 이론에 관한 고전. 달리오의 체계적이고 분산된 투자 접근법 개발과 관련

recommended.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
recommended.author: Daniel Kahneman
recommended.why: Explores cognitive biases and decision-making that complement Dalio's emphasis on systematic thinking and removing emotional bias from decisions
recommended.whyKo: 인지 편향과 의사결정을 탐구. 달리오의 체계적 사고와 의사결정에서 감정 편향 제거 강조를 보완

personalTraits.knownFor: Founder of world's largest hedge fund (Bridgewater Associates); legendary macro investor and systematic trader; author of bestseller 'Principles'; pioneer of 'radical transparency' management culture; called 'the Steve Jobs of investing' for innovative but demanding management style; early practitioner of transcendental meditation in business; revolutionary advocate for algorithm-based, systematic investing; influential thinker on economic cycles and debt crises
personalTraits.knownForKo: 세계 최대 헤지펀드(브릿지워터 어소시에이츠) 설립자. 거시경제 투자 전설 및 체계적 거래자. 베스트셀러 '원칙' 저자. '급진적 투명성' 경영 문화의 개척자. 혁신적이면서 엄격한 경영 스타일로 '투자의 스티브 잡스'라 불림. 기업 환경에서 명상 실천의 선구자. 알고리즘 기반 체계적 투자의 혁명적 옹호자. 경제사이클과 채무위기에 대한 영향력 있는 사상가
personalTraits.philanthropy: Dalio family has donated over $5 billion to the Dalio Foundation; foundation has distributed over $1 billion in charitable grants; focused on education, poverty alleviation, and systemic change; supporter of economic research and policy reform; major donor to educational institutions and initiatives focused on economic opportunity
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: 달리오 가족이 달리오 재단에 50억 달러 이상 기부. 재단은 10억 달러 이상의 자선 기금을 배분. 교육, 빈곤 완화, 체계적 변화에 초점. 경제 연구 및 정책 개혁의 지지자. 교육 기관 및 경제 기회 중심 사업의 주요 기부자
personalTraits.controversies: Bridgewater culture of 'radical transparency' criticized for creating intimidating environment where employees feared disagreement; accusations of toxic workplace despite espoused values of open communication; departures of high-level executives attributed to management style; 2020 fund performance losses and investor withdrawals raised questions about model effectiveness; history of punching boss and drinking problems in early career; significant personal wealth accumulated while promoting principles of sharing and transparency
personalTraits.controversiesKo: '급진적 투명성' 문화가 직원들이 반대 의견을 두려워하는 위협적 환경을 조성했다는 비판. 개방적 소통 가치를 표방하면서도 독성 직장 환경 지적. 고위 경영진 이탈이 경영 스타일과 연결. 2020년 펀드 성과 부진과 투자자 이탈로 모델 효과성에 의문 제기. 초기 경력의 상사 폭행과 음주 문제 역사. 공유와 투명성 원칙을 홍보하면서 막대한 개인 자산 축적

pivotalMentors.name: Marino Dallolio (father)
pivotalMentors.relationship: Parent and early role model; jazz musician and working-class provider
pivotalMentors.influence: Demonstrated persistence and creativity in pursuing passions; father's financial struggles may have motivated Dalio's intense drive to succeed; provided support during 1982 crisis by lending $4,000 to help family survive losses

pivotalMentors.name: Paul Volcker
pivotalMentors.relationship: Professional influence through Federal Reserve actions and economic policy
pivotalMentors.influence: Volcker's monetary policy decisions in early 1980s shaped markets Dalio was trading; experiences with Volcker's tight money policy contributed to Dalio's understanding of credit cycles and inflation dynamics

pivotalMentors.name: Maharishi Mahesh Yogi (meditation teacher influence)
pivotalMentors.relationship: Founder of Transcendental Meditation practice; indirect influence through TM community
pivotalMentors.influence: Introduced Dalio to transcendental meditation in 1968; meditation became cornerstone of his decision-making process and emotional regulation; directly attributed to overcoming early alcohol problems and achieving clarity in analysis

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Wakes at 6 AM; practices transcendental meditation for 20 minutes immediately upon waking followed by another 20-minute session before dinner (inspired by Beatles who practiced TM); dedicates significant time to reading economic data, market research, and analysis; engages in rigorous exercise routine emphasizing physical fitness as prerequisite for mental clarity; participates in thoughtful disagreement sessions at Bridgewater using principle of 'hitting the beach together' for candid discussion; maintains schedule balancing investment analysis with writing and thinking on economic principles; uses meditation as tool for achieving calm analytical state before major decisions
keyAnecdotes: At age 12, bought shares of Northeast Airlines for $300 and tripled his investment when the airline merged with another company, sparking lifelong passion for investing
Worked as golf caddy on Long Island where he met Wall Street professionals; conversations with these mentors at young age planted seeds for investment career
In 1974, punched his boss in the face while drunk at New Year's Eve party and was fired from Shearson Hayden Stone; marked lowest point but catalyzed personal transformation

In 1982, went broke after betting entire firm on depression that never happened; forced to lay off all employees except himself; had to borrow $4,000 from father to pay family bills; describes this as best thing that happened because it gave him humility

Built Bridgewater from two-bedroom apartment in 1975 with initial $250,000 investment; grew it to world's largest hedge fund ($150+ billion AUM) through systematic, algorithmic approach to macro investing

Credited transcendental meditation practice begun in 1968 as single most important reason for his success; practices 40 minutes daily (two 20-minute sessions); attributes meditation to both mental clarity and emotional regulation
additionalInsights.turningPoint: The catastrophic 1982 loss when his depression prediction failed and he was forced to shut down Bridgewater except for himself. This humbling failure taught him the limits of his own predictions and sparked his realization that he needed to codify decision-making into systematic, testable principles. From this point forward, he became focused on documenting explicit principles, backtesting them, and converting discretionary judgment into algorithmic systems. This transformation from failed discretionary trader to architect of systematic investing fundamentally changed his trajectory and led to decades of superior performance.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Pioneered 'radical transparency' culture demanding radical honesty and open disagreement; created environment where employees encouraged to voice honest opinions and challenge management without fear of retaliation (though practice sometimes created intimidation); 'Steve Jobs of investing' persona combines visionary thinking with high standards and demanding expectations; uses meditation and Socratic questioning to achieve calm analytical decision-making; emphasizes 'thoughtful disagreement' and 'hitting the beach together' for honest dialogue; values intellectual rigor and systematic thinking over intuition; promotes merit-based advancement based on actual performance data; focuses on identifying and developing high-quality thinking rather than loyalty to personalities; driven by principle of making Bridgewater's culture replicable and sustainable beyond himself; demands excellence and constant improvement while emphasizing that failure is necessary for growth
